name: Viennese Chocolate Torte Passover
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 250 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Have an ungreased 10-inch tube pan with a height of 4 1/2 inches.
3. Beat egg yolks with 1/2 cup sugar until very thick. Blend in melted chocolate, orange juice, and rind.
4. Mix ground walnuts with cake meal and potato starch. Fold into the yolk mixture.
5. With clean, dry beaters, beat egg whites until foamy. Gradually beat in the remaining 1/2 cup sugar until stiff peaks form.
6. Gently fold the yolk mixture into the beaten whites.
7. Turn batter into the tube pan and bake for 55 to 60 minutes, or until done.
8. Invert the pan on a rack and let the cake cool completely.
9. Remove from the pan and slice the cake in half horizontally.
10. Spread orange filling over the top of the bottom half. Place the second layer on top.
11. Coat the cake with glaze.
12. Garnish with remaining nuts and orange sections.
Orange Filling:
1. Melt preserves; stir in orange sections. Cool for 5 minutes.
Chocolate Glaze:
1. Heat orange juice. Remove from heat, add chocolate, stirring until melted and smooth. Cool for 5 minutes.
